chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ طَافَ عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 134 – The one who performs Tawaaf without being in ablution

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/45)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/45)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَحَدَهُمَا ( عليهما السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ طَافَ طَوَافَ الْفَرِيضَةِ وَ هُوَ عَلَى غَيْرِ طَهُورٍ قَالَ يَتَوَضَّأُ وَ يُعِيدُ طَوَافَهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ تَطَوُّعاً تَوَضَّأَ وَ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Ala’a Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘I asked one of the two (5th or 6th Imamasws) about a man who performs the Obligatory Tawaaf, and he is without purity. Heasws said: ‘He should perform ablution and he would repeat his Tawaaf. And if it was optional, he should perform ablution and Pray two Cycles’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn, from Safwan ibn Yahya from al-‘Ala’ ibn Razin from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I once asked one of the two Imam, (abu Ja’far or abu ‘Abd Allah), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who performs obligatory Tawaf without Tahur (cleansing). He (the Imam) said, ‘He must take wudu’ and do his Tawaf again; even if it is optional, he must take wudu’ and perform Salat (prayer).”’
